method: tiktoken cl100k_base (importable; the bytes/3.6 fallback was NOT needed — sanity check shows it would have undercounted this notation-dense text by ~12%: optables 29,613 B/3.6 = 8,226 vs 9,194 actual). Exact counts on both artifacts, split per section at headings. Sealed forms decomposed into fixed per-form prose (form rules + visitor contracts + companion enums + CG marks + OPEN-FLAGs: seq 920, table 1,149, conc_queue 1,273 tokens) plus op-table rows averaging 90 tokens/row (seq 84.5, table 90.3, conc_queue 102.0; concurrency rows run longer). Remaining kernels estimated as prose_estimate + rows x 90, prose scaled by measured analog complexity. Checked-library contracts at 50% (range 40-60% per task assumption) of a same-row-count sealed table. Cards estimated from the two measured samples (C1 = 3,014, C2 = 2,152, mean 2,583) with a two-tier split (heavy ~2,800 / light ~2,150) by card complexity. Kernel-delta per-rule cost measured from spec/kernel-spec-v0.6.md: 15,216 tokens / 90 rules = ~169 tokens/rule.
extrapolation_table: pool (~14 rows): ~2,350 (1,100 prose + 14x90; generation/handle/two-slot-borrow prose is table-class)
arena kernel (~8 rows): ~1,620 (900 + 720; brand/region cluster prose partly charged to kernel deltas)
threads+par (~16 rows): ~2,740 (1,300 + 1,440; concurrency prose runs conc_queue-heavy)
sync 5-member (~14 rows): ~2,760 (1,500 + 1,260; five members each carry a normative rule paragraph)
shared<T> (~4 rows): ~1,260 (900 + 360; epoch/grace-period prose dominates its 4 rows)
io-file (~14 rows): ~2,260 (1,000 + 1,260; per-row named-consumer + platform semantics)
SEALED KERNELS SUBTOTAL: ~23,500 (8,116 measured + ~14,330 extrapolated + 1,078 conventions)
COW-republish card: ~2,800 (heavy: normative mutation rule + bands)
CSR graph card (pass-1): ~2,800 (heavy: construction + traversal code)
timer wheel card (pass-1): ~2,800 (heavy: composition + wheel math)
appendix_total_estimate: ~65,500 tokens central estimate (kernels ~23.5k + library contracts ~5.0k + cards ~37.0k); honest range ~58k-71k. Even the optimistic low end exceeds the 55k FAIL line; the cards are ~56% of the total.
kernel_delta_note: The ~60-75 sealed-form kernel-delta rules are budgeted separately. Measured baseline: kernel-spec-v0.6.md is 15,216 tokens for 90 rules = ~169 tokens/rule, so 60-75 delta rules cost ~10.1-12.7k additional spec tokens. Combined picture: appendix ~65.5k + deltas ~11.4k = ~75-78k of new normative mass, i.e. the appendix alone already rivals and the combined package clearly exceeds the entire existing 90-rule kernel spec (15.2k) by ~5x. Even a successfully reduced 40k appendix plus deltas lands at ~50-53k combined — the D2 spec-compactness constraint is under real pressure from the catalog, not from the kernel deltas, and any reduction plan should treat the delta budget as effectively fixed (rules compress poorly) and take all savings from the appendix side, cards first.
reduction_levers: 1. Card example code -> teaching corpus (OWNER DECISION, load-bearing and the single biggest swing): code blocks + misuse snippets are 713/3,014 (24%) of C1 and 785/2,152 (36%) of C2; with C1's embedded op-table included, non-prose card mass is 43% of measured card tokens. Moving card code (and keeping only signatures + the one-line ordering rules) cuts cards from ~37k to ~22k and the total to ~50.5k. This alone moves FAIL -> REDUCE, not PASS, so it must combine with lever 2 or 3; whether card code counts against the appendix or the teaching corpus must be ruled explicitly before any band is re-run.

2. De-duplicate op rows restated inside cards: C1 re-quotes 11 pool/table rows (~717 tokens, in a different pass-1 row style than Appendix S — a drift hazard as well as a cost). Replace with rule-ID citations ([POOL-x], [TBL-x]) per DIAG-1 discipline. If ~half the 15 cards carry a similar context table, this saves ~4-6k and removes a normative-text fork.

3. Shared column defaults in the nine-column row format: measured rows average 90 tokens, but 'loan: NONE', 'kills: —', 'cg: —', 'own: —' and the repeated failure boilerplate are the bulk of many rows. A CAT-1 default-elision convention (a row states only non-default columns) plausibly brings the average to ~60-65 tokens/row; across ~131 rows-equivalent (85-100 sealed + library contract rows) that saves ~3-4k.

4. Named shared spellings for repeated failure/fact clauses: the clause 'trap "seq index out of bounds"; elidable by INB(s, i) proof' pattern appears 8+ times in seq alone, and the elidable-by-HAS pattern 4+ times in table. Defining ELIDE-INB(i)/ELIDE-HAS(k)/TRAP-OOB macros in S.0 plus trimming the per-card 'Replaces'/rhetorical prose (~1,400-1,600 prose tokens per card; a 25% prose trim across 15 cards is ~5k) saves ~6-7k combined.

5. Drop or defer marginal rows: spsc-conditional rows (already keyed to the necessity sweep — if spsc defers, ~3-4 conc_queue rows and CG-QOP spsc text go with it), symmetric try/blocking batch pairs that could share one row with a [blocks] flag column, and seq_swap/seq_replace-class convenience rows expressible as taught two-row sequences. ~10-15 rows x 90 = only ~1-1.5k — the smallest lever, because the row set is already D16-trimmed; spend it last.
caveats: Card sample is 2 of 15 and both are flagship-complexity compositions; if the eight pass-1 cards are systematically lighter than C2, the cards subtotal could drop toward ~30k — still leaving the total in the REDUCE band at best without the code-split lever.

Library op-count equivalents (btree ~16, bytescan ~12, sort ~8, layout ~12, wal ~8 rows-eq) are my assumptions — the task and CATALOG-V1-RECUT.md give no per-library row counts; the 40-60% contract-density factor is the task's assumption, not a measurement.

Remaining-kernel row counts (pool 14, arena 8, threads+par 16, sync 14, shared 4, io-file 14, extern-C 6 = 76 rows) are planning estimates; the recut's own headline says ~85-100 sealed rows total vs my 53 measured + 76 assumed = 129, so either some assumed counts shrink in authoring (pulling my kernel subtotal down ~1-2k) or the recut undercounts.
Concurrency-heavy kernels (threads+par, sync) may exceed the 90-token/row average the way conc_queue did (102/row); my prose uplifts partially compensate but the kernel subtotal could run ~10% hot.

cl100k_base is a proxy; the tokenizer of whichever model actually consumes the appendix will differ by a few percent (o200k-class tokenizers typically count ~5-10% fewer on this kind of text). The bytes/3.6 approximation was not used, and would have undercounted by ~12%.
The C1 embedded op-table uses pass-1 row style (present(t,*), live(p,h)) that does not match Appendix S notation (HAS/ELEM) — beyond cost, this is a consistency defect the dedup lever also fixes.

Counts are of the artifacts exactly as authored, including OPEN-FLAG text; if discharged flags delete their OPEN-FLAG paragraphs before ratification, ~600-900 tokens come back across the three measured kernels.
